title: Sensor Failure
brief: You've tasked the primary radar when it fails. MS raises an MA_FaultMT and the primary stops collecting — it will never report. Don't keep waiting on a dead sensor: cancel the task, re-task the healthy backup radar, and finish the collection.
teaches: MS has its own fault model, and it speaks the same MA_FaultMT as FA — the same message type on a different bus (MS Vol §1.2.2.4, lie #19). When a sensor degrades, the standing task is dead: cancel it (AMTI_CommandMT CommandState CANCEL) and re-task a healthy capability. A brain that ignores the fault keeps waiting on silence and times out.

objective._doc: No flight. Win = two tracks (EntityMT) collected. The primary degrades at tick 5 before its track (offset 6) ever fires, so it reports nothing; MA_FaultMT arrives at t6. Cancelling the primary and tasking AESA-BACKUP-1 collects both backup tracks. Ignoring the fault collects nothing and times out.
